summary: George Russell fended off Max Verstappen's sustained pressure over 71 laps to deliver Mercedes its first victory of the season, capitalizing on the pole position he seized with a controversial final-lap effort in qualifying. The battle intensified in the closing stages as Verstappen hunted Russell through the heat-hazard conditions at the Red Bull Ring, but the Mercedes driver's tire management proved decisive in the sweltering Austrian heatwave. The win narrows Red Bull's championship lead as Mercedes signals a resurgence in performance after a difficult start to the campaign.
